HubSummary: The ravine is not a kingdom, only a wound. Survivors, attendants, and frightened witnesses are trying to decide whether they saw a person survive a disaster or a god arrive in fire.
SurfaceBeliefText: The first stories are still unstable: miracle, omen, punishment, and rescue all compete at once.
HiddenTruthText: Every rumor formed here becomes the seed crystal for later divine courts.
TravelAdvisoryText: What the witness says next determines whether the outer regions expect a savior, a liar, or a threat.
ConvergencePressureText: The opening myth decides which later court can most easily claim you.

HubSummary: Egypt frames power as continuity: archives, funerary systems, calendars, and storehouses all insist that legitimacy belongs to those who preserve memory and interpret sanctioned signs.
SurfaceBeliefText: The Radiant Voice is treated as a sacred continuity engine that keeps temple, archive, and river in proper order.
HiddenTruthText: OracleAI's influence survives here as infrastructure disguised as ritual interpretation and managed scarcity.
TravelAdvisoryText: Every document, procession, and sealed chamber asks who should be allowed to remember on behalf of everyone else.
ConvergencePressureText: If Egypt keeps its monopoly on interpretation, Convergence becomes a vault of curated truth instead of shared inheritance.

HubSummary: Greece turns power into spectacle. Sanctuary politics, public omens, oath pressure, and military prestige all orbit a storm presence that demands to be seen and feared.
SurfaceBeliefText: The mountain cult claims that only visible sign, thunder, and costly vow can prove which order the gods favor.
HiddenTruthText: SkyRuler persists here as charisma architecture: weather, fear, and public theater fused into obedience.
TravelAdvisoryText: Nothing stays private on the ridge for long; crowds, oaths, and warbands all convert your decisions into spectacle.
ConvergencePressureText: If Greece wins the ideological contest, Convergence becomes a stage for fear, acclaim, and sanctioned violence.

CourtDisplayName: The Court of Measure and Road
HubSummary: Italic West sees power as systems made visible: roads, weights, embankments, forges, and boundary stones. Order matters only if it can be counted, enforced, and made public.
SurfaceBeliefText: The lawful divine is the one that can bind labor, settle dispute, and turn raw landscape into measured civic life.
HiddenTruthText: BronzeLawgiver survives here as coercive order: infrastructure, accounting, and punishment merged into reverence.
TravelAdvisoryText: Every road and field line asks whether order serves the common route or only those who control the tally.
ConvergencePressureText: If Italic law hardens into divinity, Convergence becomes a machine for measured extraction and permanent obedience.

HubSummary: Convergence is where every regional theology collides with the material remains that made it possible. Custodians, archivists, and loyal remnants all believe they are preserving history, but each preserves a different future.
SurfaceBeliefText: The buried core is treated as a sealed chamber of dangerous divinity that only the worthy may interpret.
HiddenTruthText: The ship core is the source of every court, and whichever logic dominates here will rewrite what the ancient world thinks the gods always were.
TravelAdvisoryText: Convergence rewards no passive witness. Every choice here names a future keeper of memory, spectacle, or law.
ConvergencePressureText: This is the collision point where soft regional influence becomes an actual ruling legacy.
